textPlain: How is this fundamentally any different than Erlang/Elixir concepts of supervisors controlling their child processes? It seems like the AI industry keeps re-discovering several basic techniques that have been around since the 80s.I'm not surprised—most AI "engineers" are not really good software engineers; they're often "vibe engineers" who don't read academic papers on the subject and keep re-inventing the wheel.If someone asked me why I think there's an AI bubble, I'd point exactly to this situation.
